lede: A look at September 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lance Armstrong and Greta Garbo, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Anthrax
events.desc: First mailing of anthrax letters from Trenton, New Jersey in the 2001 anthrax attacks.

events.title: Wedding of Constantine II and Princess Anne-Marie
events.desc: The wedding of Constantine II of Greece and Princess Anne-Marie of Denmark takes place in Athens.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 213
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 213 crashes into a mountain near Chersky Airport, killing 32 people.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: U.S. Eighth Army and United Nations forces break out of the Pusan Perimeter in southeast Korea.

events.title: Netherlands
events.desc: In the Netherlands, a law granting full voting rights to women is granted royal assent by Queen Wilhelmina.

events.title: 1885 Pierce City lynching
events.desc: Five Chinese people were lynched outside of Pierce City in the Idaho Territory of the United States.

events.title: Blackpool Illuminations
events.desc: The Blackpool Illuminations are switched on for the first time.

events.title: The New York Times
events.desc: First publication of The New-York Daily Times, which later becomes The New York Times.

events.title: Constantine the Great
events.desc: Constantine the Great decisively defeats Licinius in the Battle of Chrysopolis, establishing Constantine's sole control over the Roman Empire.

events.title: AD 96
events.desc: Emperor Domitian is assassinated as a result of a plot by his wife Domitia and two Praetorian prefects. Nerva is then proclaimed as his successor.

births.name: Edwin McMillan
births.line: American physicist and chemist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: James Scullin
births.line: Australian journalist and politician, 9th Prime Minister of Australia

births.name: Pope Gregory XVI
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1831 to 1846

births.name: John McAfee
births.line: British-American computer programmer and businessman, founded McAfee

deaths.text: Ruth Bader Ginsburg, United States Supreme Court justice
Jimi Hendrix, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and producer
Dag Hammarskjöld, Swedish economist and diplomat, 2nd Secretary-General of the United Nations, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Edward Bouverie Pusey (Episcopal Church)
